text: Paintball Act 2018. Use this form to apply for a new paintball marker permit or the renewal of an existing paintball marker permit. A paintball marker permit authorises an individual to: purchase or otherwise obtain possession of one or more paintball markers; and possess one or more paintball markers other than at an authorised paintball venue. Eligibility: You must be at least 18 years of age and must have completed the NSW Paintball Marker Safety Training course.

You must make payment online via the NSW Government payment portal before submitting your application. Please record the receipt number below before lodging — your application will take longer to assess if you do not provide this information.

label: Receipt Number

text: IMPORTANT — Are your details correct? NSW Fair Trading must be notified within 14 days of any changes occurring to the licensee's name, address, or other registered particulars.

label: Application Type
Existing Permit Number (if renewing)
Permit Duration

text: If you are a new applicant, you must attach one form of identity documentation to your application.

label: Surname
Given Name
Former Names (if any, including maiden name)
Residential Address (not PO Box)
Email Address (all correspondence will be sent to this address)
Phone Number
Mobile Number

text: Only complete this section if you are applying for a new permit. If you are renewing your permit, proceed to Section 4.

label: I confirm that I have completed the NSW Paintball Marker Safety Training course

text: Only complete this section if you are renewing a permit. If your address has changed, please provide the storage details of your paintball markers. Note: If the paintball marker does not have a serial number, provide a description of the paintball marker. Attach more pages if required.

text: If you answer Yes to any of the below questions, you must complete the Paintball Permit Additional Details form and submit it as an attachment to this application.

label: Have you been convicted of any offence in the last 10 years (new applicants) OR since your last application (renewal applicants)?
Have you had an equivalent authorisation suspended or cancelled (other than on your request) under the law of another Australian jurisdiction?

text: Note: An equivalent authorisation includes the following licences issued in respect of a paintball marker: (a) an adult firearms licence in the category of paintball marker under the Firearms Act 1996 of the ACT; (b) a licence for a category A weapon under the Weapons Act 1990 of QLD; (c) a licence for a category A weapon under the Firearms Act 2015 of SA; (d) a paintball marker licence under the Firearms Act 1996 of Victoria; (e) a category A firearms licence under the Firearms Act 1996 of Tasmania; (f) a licence for a subcategory E5 firearm under the Firearms Act 1973 of WA.

label: Have you been disqualified (other than on grounds of not residing in the relevant jurisdiction) from holding an equivalent authorisation under the law of another Australian jurisdiction?
Do you have any mental incapacity that would impact your ability to hold a permit in NSW?
Are you an involuntary, forensic or correctional patient within the meaning of the Mental Health Act 2007 (NSW)?
Are you a protected person within the meaning of the NSW Trustee and Guardian Act 2009 (NSW)?

text:
I certify that the particulars specified in this application and all attachments are, to the best of my knowledge, true and correct in every detail.

Statement under the Privacy and Personal Information Protection Act 1998. The applicant for this licence: (1) authorises NSW Fair Trading to make any inquiries and to receive and disclose any information which is relevant to the applicant's initial and ongoing eligibility to hold this licence; (2) accepts that failure to supply information required on this application form may delay the processing of the application; and (3) has a right to seek access to and correction of information supplied.

Making a false or misleading statement, giving false or misleading information, or producing false or misleading documents is a serious offence which may render you liable to prosecution for offences including under the Crimes Act 1900 which may result in penalties including a fine or imprisonment.

label: I confirm that I am authorised to provide the personal details presented
I consent to my information being checked with the document issuer for the purpose of confirming my identity

Date (DD/MM/YYYY)

text: Paintball Permits – Privacy Collection Notice. Your information is being collected by the Department of Customer Service on behalf of the Commissioner for NSW Fair Trading, in accordance with the Privacy and Personal Information Protection Act 1998 (PPIP Act) and Health Records and Information Privacy Act 2002 (HRIP Act). NSW Fair Trading collects your personal information for the purpose of assessing your application to apply for, update, renew or replace a paintball permit in line with the Paintball Act 2018 and the Paintball Regulation 2024. We may collect your full name, contact information, details of previous permits, third party details, proof of identity documents, details of relevant offences and pending charges in the last ten years, financial information including bankruptcy, and details of mental capacity where applicable. Your information is provided voluntarily however if you choose not to provide it, we may be unable to process your application. We may share your information with NSW Government agencies (including NSW Police Force), other States, Territories and/or the Commonwealth for compliance purposes, and with Service NSW and Digital NSW where this form is lodged at a Service NSW Centre or online. You have the right to request access to and correction of your personal information. For privacy enquiries or complaints contact FTPrivacy@customerservice.nsw.gov.au.
